TextENGNew: When I asked Rika-chan, she said Satoko might still be on the mountain behind the shrine.
Satoko loved it up there.
She loved that mountain—dense with trees, tangled with vines, littered with fallen logs, and rich enough to offer akebi berries to eat.
changeReason: Old: 'covered in with trees, vines, and fallen logs, and she really likes the area'; New: 'dense with trees, tangled with vines, littered with fallen logs, and rich enough to offer akebi berries to eat'. Old omits the akebi berries (アケビも食べられる) entirely.

TextENGNew: A secret base—the dream of every child.
...And for Satoko, the whole mountain was her secret base.
But it was undeveloped wilderness, and easy enough to get lost in—a genuinely dangerous place.
...As her brother, I had some reservations about her using a place like that as a playground, but
...if anything, I—who barely ever came here—was far more likely to lose my way than Satoko, who played on this mountain all the time.
The forest gets dark quickly when evening comes.
If I kept searching and still could not find her, I would have to look somewhere else.
Satoko!! Satoko!!
changeReason: Old: uses backtick quotes as dialogue; New: removes dialogue formatting to plain text. The presentation changes from quoted dialogue to narrative voice, but the words are the same.

TextENGNew: I was calling out as loudly as I could, but voices do not carry well in the forest.
According to Rika-chan, she had been building a secret base somewhere around here recently—going out alone to expand and develop it.
Expand a base... what did that mean exactly...?
If she called it a secret base, she would not want anyone to find it.
Did that mean she was setting up traps to keep people away...?
...If I had only thought it through that far, I might have noticed what Satoko had been putting in this mountain—before one of them got me.
changeReason: Old: 'Considering that, I should have noticed the traps on the mountain before one got me'; New: 'If I had only thought it through that far, I might have noticed what Satoko had been putting in this mountain—before one of them got me'. Old implies he did realize but was too late; new suggests he failed to think it through — different implication of awareness.

TextENGNew: Wh-whoa?!
Suddenly my foot plunged down into the ground, catching on something like a jump rope concealed beneath dry leaves.
From the trees overhead came a rustling, crackling commotion—and then, right beside me, something like a large ball made of bundled dead branches came crashing down.
...It could only be one of the trap games Satoko loved.
If the mechanism had worked exactly as intended, it would have landed squarely on my head.
changeReason: Old: 'If I wasn't so lucky, I'm sure the ball would have hit my head'; New: 'If the mechanism had worked exactly as intended, it would have landed squarely on my head'. Old attributes escape to luck; new attributes it to mechanism malfunction — different cause.

TextENGNew:
...Oh my.
Why, who might this be!
Nii-nii, is it.

Satoko...!!
This kind of thing is dangerous—you really should not be doing this...!

There was a sign put up by the forestry service saying it is dangerous to enter, was there not?
Anyone who ignores that warning is greeted by a splendid welcome from my traps.
Oh ho ho ho!
...I should have reprimanded her as her brother—dangerous games are dangerous.
...But seeing Satoko's bright smile—a smile I had not seen in so long—I found I could not say another word.

Satoko,
we were told to come home by evening today—the whole family is going out for dinner.

...I don't want to go.
Our mother and father can go by themselves.
If you want to go, you can go with them—just the three of you.
......Are you still angry at Father?
``.........He has been unpleasant to me again.``
changeReason: Old fabricates 'He threw an ashtray at me' while JPN says 'He has been unpleasant to me again.' Invented violent action.

TextENGNew: "...That's not right.@k@vS20/07/VTK1_satoshi1148. You're overthinking it, Satoko.@k@vS20/07/VTK1_satoshi1149. Our stepfather is worried about you."
changeReason: Old fabricates dialogue distinguishing 'our previous father' vs 'our current one' while JPN says 'That's not right, you're overthinking it, our stepfather is worried about you.'

TextENGNew: "...He said horrible things to me.@k@vS20/04/VTK1_satoko1013. He gets angry over the slightest thing—he scolded me harshly for being a picky eater!@k@vS20/04/VTK1_satoko1014. And then, and then...!!"
changeReason: Old fabricates specific abuse details ('locked on veranda, didn't feed lunch, wrong brand of cigarettes, slapped face') not in JPN, which describes verbal abuse and harsh scolding for picky eating.

TextENGNew: ...None of that was about our current stepfather.
She had jumbled together memories of several different stepfathers.
Every time our mother remarried, we had been made to call a different person Father.
...The word father is not one to be used lightly.
...Unless you truly acknowledged someone as your father from the bottom of your heart, you could not even say it as a joke—that was how much weight the word carried.
Even so, I had enough sense to say it—if only to help smooth over the awkwardness of the family situation.
......But to ask Satoko, at her age, to do the same was simply too much.
From the time she was very small, Satoko had clashed with one stepfather after another—and with our current one, she had never opened her heart even from their very first meeting.
For Satoko... a father figure was no longer something she could trust.
It was true that our current stepfather had never had any experience raising children of his own.
Because of that, there had been times when he dealt with Satoko in a way that was overly emotional.
But ever since the incident when Satoko reported him to the child welfare centre, he had reflected on his behaviour and was making a genuine, if modest, effort to connect with her.
changeReason: Old: 'Satoko called the child abuse hotline'; New: 'Satoko reported him to the child welfare centre'. Japanese 生活相談所 is a life/welfare consultation centre, not specifically a child abuse hotline.

TextENGNew: ...My mother too had been advised by the welfare officer about the emotional wounds Satoko carried, and was slowly groping her way toward a way of relating to her as family.
changeReason: Old: 'Even my mother realized how much Satoko has suffered emotionally when it was pointed out by the social worker. She's doing her part'; New: 'My mother too had been advised by the welfare officer about the emotional wounds Satoko carried, and was slowly groping her way toward a way of relating to her as family'. Japanese 保護司 is a probation/welfare officer, and the text emphasizes the slow, searching quality of the mother's efforts.

TextENGNew: ...And yet Satoko would not accept it.
......It was understandable, all the same.
Her childhood had been steeped in too much sadness...
Satoko... would probably only open her heart to me.
That was why I—as her only true blood relation—had to be the one to stand by her. If I were not there... Satoko would have no family left in this world...
But a brother and sister alone is a relationship—it is not what you call a family.
However stiff and provisional a family it might be... if we kept building small connections, one at a time... the day would come when real bonds of family would form between us...

Satoko...
Our stepfather has been getting kinder lately.
...He says he wants to reconcile with you.
...I have never heard him say that.

That is because every time he tries to say something, you run away.
He wants to talk with you more.
...He said he would not force you to eat pumpkin any more.

...That is a lie.
He kept telling me I could not leave the table until I had eaten my pumpkin.
...That was not about our current stepfather either...

Satoko.
Anyway—will you come home with me?
Mother won those dinner vouchers in a draw—it would be a shame to waste them.
It is reservation only, so if we do not go today, they will be cancelled.

...You can go without me, Nii-nii.
I prefer staying home alone.
Satoko did not usually stay out playing this late.
...She had known beforehand that today was supposed to be a family outing—and that was precisely why she had deliberately stayed alone on the mountain until this hour.
Part of me desperately wanted to snap at her to stop being so stubborn—but I forced that impatience back down.
...I was the only person Satoko could rely on.
...I must not frighten her...

Anyway, Satoko—let's go home?
You don't have to decide about the dinner right now. You can make up your mind once we're back.
...Besides, if you don't show me the way,
I have no idea how to get home.

...Nii-nii...
I have brought you here so many times already, and you still have not learned the way?
Goodness, you really are hopeless, aren't you, Nii-nii.
Satoko held out her hand.
She usually said that holding Nii-nii's hand in front of people was embarrassing—and yet here she was, reaching out first.
I took her hand.
Satoko's slender little hand.
......She still needed me to protect her.
Let's go home, Satoko.
......All right.
